text.title: Jira Workflow Design
text.summary: Rules for configuring Jira issue types, workflow states/transitions, and board setup to create maintainable, scalable project structures.

section.title: Issue Types

rule.statement: Use one workflow scheme per issue type before creating project-scoped variants.
rule.rationale: Workflow schemes are shared across projects; a per-project variant multiplies maintenance cost and risks breaking sibling projects when schemes are edited.

section.p: Standard issue types and their required fields:

list.item: Epic — Summary, Description, Start/End Date; groups related Stories
Story — Story Points, Acceptance Criteria; user-facing functionality
Task — Estimate, Component; technical work with no user story framing
Bug — Severity, Steps to Reproduce; defect with reproduction path
Sub-task — linked to parent; used only when a Story exceeds 1-day granularity

rule.statement: Limit workflow statuses to 5-7 maximum per issue type.
rule.rationale: More statuses increase cognitive load and transition errors without improving visibility. Reflect actual team process, not an ideal one.

example.code:
statuses: - name: "To Do" category: "To Do" - name: "In Progress" category: "In Progress" - name: "Code Review" category: "In Progress" - name: "QA Testing" category: "In Progress" - name: "Done" category: "Done"
transitions: - from: "To Do" to: "In Progress" trigger: "Start Work" - from: "In Progress" to: "Code Review" trigger: "Submit for Review" condition: "Branch linked" - from: "Code Review" to: "QA Testing" trigger: "Approve" - from: "QA Testing" to: "Done" trigger: "Pass QA"
example.note: Transitions use validators (e.g., "Branch linked") to enforce process gates without extra communication overhead.

rule.statement: Add required-field validators on transitions, not on issue creation.
rule.rationale: Requiring fields at creation time increases friction for bulk imports and bot-driven flows. Gate fields at the transition where they become meaningful (e.g., Code Reviewer at "Submit for Review").

rule.statement: Set WIP limits on Kanban columns; use swimlanes to group by epic or priority on Scrum boards.
rule.rationale: WIP limits make overload visible. Swimlanes reduce context switching by grouping related work during daily standups.

list.item: Scrum board: backlog + active sprint + burndown chart + velocity chart
Kanban board: WIP limits per column + cumulative flow diagram + cycle time tracking
Quick filters: "My Issues", "Blocked", "No Assignee" — accessible in one click
Backlog grooming target: keep under 2 sprints worth of refined stories

section.title: Custom Fields

rule.statement: Share custom fields via Global context; avoid project-scoped contexts where possible.
rule.rationale: Project-scoped custom fields compound across projects and hit Jira's 200-field issue-view cap, slowing the entire site for all users.

section.reference: Custom field definitions with type and screen placement
